name: Auto-post the content calendar with Airtable and WordPress

parameters.text: =✅ Auto-post the content calendar with Airtable and WordPress — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Daily Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — daily at 9:00. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Read Source Feed

### Read Source Feed

Pulls the RSS feed and emits one item per entry (title, link, content).

Ports: ①→ Skip Already-Seen Posts · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Skip Already-Seen Posts

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Keep Top Stories

### Keep Top Stories

Caps the stream at 5 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Rewrite for Our Audience (AI)

### Rewrite for Our Audience (AI)

Single fixed LLM step (no tool choice) — cheaper and more predictable than an agent.

Ports: ①→ Publish to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### OpenAI Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `gpt-4o-mini`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Rewrite for Our Audience (AI)** as `languageModel`

🔑 OpenAI API key

### Publish to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#content`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Log Published Posts (Airtable) · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Log Published Posts (Airtable)

Creates a new record in Airtable's 'Drafts' table.

Ports: ①→ Align Field Names · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 100 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Guard Non-Empty topic

### Guard Non-Empty topic

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Airtable)

### Append Audit Row (Airtable)

Creates a new record in Airtable's 'Drafts' table.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#content`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#content`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Auto-post the content calendar with Airtable and WordPress

**Pattern:** Social Scheduler · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Auto-post the content calendar with Airtable and WordPress. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual blog posts is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule → Read Source Feed → Skip Already-Seen Posts → Keep Top Stories → Rewrite for Our Audience (AI) → Publish to Slack → Log Published Posts (Airtable) → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to social scheduler without manual effort
- Anyone automating blog posts in content creation
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Removes copy-paste errors

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Airtable PAT; OpenAI API key; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Deduplication is cross-execution, not just within a single run — an RSS feed re-serves the same items on every poll, and without this the same story would get posted again on every scheduled check.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Turn off the AI rewrite step and compare the raw feed text against the rewritten version.
2. Add a second RSS source and merge both into one feed before dedupe.
3. Change 'Keep Top Stories' from 5 to 1 and see how it changes posting cadence.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your blog posts process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
